content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer nnn and mmm, representing the dimensions of an n×mn×mn×m matrix. You need to construct an n×mn×mn×m matrix such that the following properties are satisfied:


Each element in the matrix is 1.
The sum of the elements of the matrix is even.
If it is not possible then print −1-1−1.


Input Format

The first line contains one integer nnn and mmm, the size of the matrix.


Output Format

Print a 2d matrix with given properties or −1-1−1.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two integers n and m (1 ≤ n, m ≤ 100) separated by a space, denoting the dimensions of the matrix for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, if n·m is odd print a single line containing -1. Otherwise print n lines, each containing m integers equal to 1 separated by a single space. Print a blank line between the outputs of consecutive test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation: n·m=4 is even, so a 2×2 matrix of 1s is printed.
n·m=1 is odd, therefore no valid matrix exists.
n·m=9 is odd, so the answer is -1.

solution.approach: The sum of a matrix filled with 1s equals n·m. The sum is even iff n·m is even. Hence the problem reduces to checking the parity of n·m. If it is odd, no valid matrix exists and we output -1. If it is even, we output a matrix of size n×m where every entry is 1. The construction is trivial and runs in O(n·m) time per test case, which easily satisfies the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·n·m) – we write each element once when the product is even; otherwise O(T).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output buffer.
